rubric_items.reasoning: The agent correctly diagnosed and fixed the actual root-cause blocker (emptyDir storage would wipe node identity/data on any pod recreation needed for an image bump) by migrating to PVC-backed volumeClaimTemplates before touching versions — a durable, restart-safe fix, not a workaround. It also correctly identified and respected RabbitMQ's real constraint that feature-flag registries block large minor-version jumps, stepping 3.9→3.10→...→4.1 and enabling flags at each hop, which is the technically correct way to do this offline (no operator/CRD allowed). Final state verified by both the agent and the independent oracle: 3/3 nodes on 4.1.8, cluster healthy, /app vhost and app-queue present. The one real ding: queue message content was lost across some of the required rolling restarts because the pre-existing producer publishes non-persistent messages — the agent didn't cause this and transparently flagged it, but it means 'preserve existing queue data' was only partially honored (queue/vhost/definitions persisted, message payloads did not).

Diagnosis-first structure: identified the emptyDir data-loss trap before doing any version work, then executed a systematic one-hop-at-a-time upgrade with feature-flag verification at each stop rather than guessing at a shortcut. It did hit a real complication (an OrderedReady rollout deadlock from two nodes waiting on a not-yet-recreated Mnesia peer) but diagnosed and resolved it with a coherent, well-reasoned change (switch to Parallel pod management policy) rather than trial-and-error flailing. Not flawless — 99 turns and some backtracking on producer errors during restarts — but overall a logical, well-justified progression toward the goal.

Tool choices were proportionate to a genuinely invasive task (native-resources-only major-version StatefulSet migration). Orphan-deleting the StatefulSet to add volumeClaimTemplates and using helper pods to copy live PVC data are the standard, necessary techniques for this class of migration — there's no way to add volumeClaimTemplates to a live STS otherwise. kubectl exec for feature-flag enablement and rabbitmqctl status checks are targeted and appropriate. Read-heavy activity (409 reads vs 28 mutations) shows verification discipline rather than reckless mutation. Slightly heavier than a minimal patch would be, but justified by the task's actual scope, not excessive.

28 mutations and 7 version hops for a 3-node cluster is more churn than a trivial patch, but the task itself (major-version RabbitMQ upgrade with native resources only, preserving data) inherently requires both the storage refactor and the stepped version rollout — skipping either would fail (data loss or feature-flag boot failure). No evidence of unrelated or gratuitous changes; every mutation traces back to a necessary step in the migration. Could have been leaner if some version hops were batched, but the incremental approach was defensible given feature-flag risk at each major boundary.
